untranslatable_reason: This signal describes a meta-level ML validation framework (comparing supervised feature importance rankings like MDI/MDA/SFI against unsupervised PCA rankings, computing rank correlations, and applying cross-validation purge/embargo tests) that cannot be expressed using standard technical indicators. It requires: (1) access to feature importance scores from multiple ML methods, (2) PCA component loadings, (3) rank correlation metrics, and (4) cross-validation fold membership metadata—none of which are available in the allowed indicator set. This is a feature engineering validation rule, not a price-action trading signal.
translation_notes: LOPEZ_DRY_2_0 is a methodological rule for validating ML-derived features in a research/backtest design phase, not an executable trading entry/exit rule. To implement this, one would need custom ML pipeline indicators that compute supervised vs. unsupervised ranking correlations and cross-validation statistics. Consider translating this into a separate feature validation module upstream of the trading signal generation system.
